journey.name: Login & Dashboard Flow
journey.description: End-to-end customer journey covering registration, email verification, duplicate-email validation, authentication, profile management, and session lifecycle.

steps.name: Register new account
steps.description: Register a brand new loyalty account with a randomly generated name and email address. Validates the full three-step orchestration: Customer MS record creation, Identity MS account creation, and record linking.

body.message: Registration successful. Please check your email to verify your account.

expected.description: 201 Created — registration successful. Response contains the newly issued loyalty number, the customer's given name, initial tier (always Blue), and a confirmation message directing the user to verify their email.
expected.validationNotes: loyaltyNumber starts with 'AX' and is 9 characters. tierCode is always 'Blue' for new registrations. message contains email verification instructions. Name and email are runtime-generated values unique to this test run.

steps.name: Search customer by email
steps.description: Call the Identity MS login endpoint with the registered email and password to retrieve the userAccountId. This UUID is required for the verify-email path parameter and is not returned by the Loyalty API registration response.

apiCall.note: Called directly on the Identity MS (not via the Loyalty API) to obtain the userAccountId needed for Step 3.

expected.description: 200 OK — Identity MS returns the userAccountId alongside the JWT tokens. Only userAccountId is chained forward; the tokens from this step are discarded (a fresh login via the Loyalty API is performed in Step 5).
expected.validationNotes: userAccountId is the primary key of the identity.UserAccount record. It is distinct from identityReference (the cross-domain link stored on the Customer record). The returned accessToken and refreshToken are not used in subsequent steps.

steps.name: Verify email
steps.description: Call the verify-email GET endpoint, simulating a user clicking the confirmation link sent after registration. No authentication is required. The userAccountId is chained from Step 2.

apiCall.note: No auth required — public endpoint. userAccountId is chained from Step 2 (Identity MS login); the fallback UUID is used if no live chain is available.

expected.description: 200 OK — email verified, account activated. No response body. The Identity MS sets IsEmailVerified to 1 for the account.
expected.validationNotes: Idempotent — calling again on an already-verified account still returns 200. A 404 means the userAccountId was not found.

steps.name: Duplicate registration attempt
steps.description: Attempt to register again using the same email address generated in Step 1. Validates that the API correctly rejects duplicate accounts with a 409 Conflict response.

body.error: An account with this email already exists.

expected.description: 409 Conflict — the email registered in Step 1 is already taken. Response body contains an error message. The existing account is not modified.
expected.validationNotes: Confirms idempotency guard on registration. Uses the exact same email as Step 1. The response must not reveal that the email belongs to an existing account — only a generic conflict message is permitted.

steps.name: Login
steps.description: Authenticate with the credentials registered in Step 1. Validates JWT issuance from the Identity MS and loyalty number resolution from the Customer MS.

expected.description: 200 OK — credentials validated. Response contains a short-lived JWT access token (15 min TTL), a single-use refresh token, expiry timestamp, token type, and the customer's loyalty number.
expected.validationNotes: accessToken is a signed JWT (RS256). refreshToken is opaque and single-use. loyaltyNumber starts with 'AX' and is 9 characters. expiresAt is ISO 8601 UTC, approximately 15 minutes from now.

steps.name: Token refresh
steps.description: Exchange the single-use refresh token from login for a new access token and refresh token pair. The old refresh token is revoked.

expected.description: 200 OK — new token pair issued. The previous refresh token is now invalid (single-use rotation).
expected.validationNotes: New accessToken replaces the one from Step 5. New refreshToken must be used for any subsequent refresh. Old refreshToken from Step 5 is revoked and cannot be reused.

steps.name: View profile
steps.description: Retrieve the authenticated customer's loyalty profile using the loyalty number from login and the refreshed access token.

expected.description: 200 OK — full profile returned. Tier defaults to 'Blue' for new members. Points balance starts at 0.
expected.validationNotes: loyaltyNumber matches the value from login. tier is one of: Blue, Silver, Gold, Platinum. pointsBalance is a decimal. memberSince is ISO 8601 UTC.

steps.name: View transactions
steps.description: Retrieve the transaction history for the authenticated customer. Shows point earn/redeem activity with pagination.

expected.description: 200 OK — paginated transaction history. Each transaction includes type, point delta (positive for earn, negative for redeem), running balance, and optional booking/flight references.
expected.validationNotes: transactionType is one of: Earn, Redeem, Adjustment, Expiry, Reinstate. pointsDelta is positive for credits, negative for debits. balanceAfter is the running balance after each transaction. A new account may return an empty transactions array with totalCount 0.

steps.name: Update profile
steps.description: Patch the customer's profile with updated fields. Only the provided fields are modified; omitted fields remain unchanged.

expected.description: 204 No Content — profile updated successfully. No response body. A subsequent GET /profile would reflect the changes.
expected.validationNotes: Only permitted fields are accepted: givenName, surname, dateOfBirth, nationality, phoneNumber, preferredLanguage. Fields like tier, points, identityId are ignored. Partial update — omitted fields are not cleared.

steps.name: Logout
steps.description: Invalidate the session by revoking the refresh token. The access token remains valid until its TTL expires (~15 min).

expected.description: 200 OK — refresh token revoked. Session effectively terminated. The access token is still technically valid until its 15-minute TTL expires, but no new tokens can be obtained.
expected.validationNotes: Attempting to use the revoked refresh token for a subsequent /auth/refresh call should return 401 Unauthorized.
